title: fraxinus pringlei
intro: Fraxinus pringlei, also known as the Pringle ash, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Oleaceae family. Traditionally, Fraxinus pringlei has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Fraxinus pringlei include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting respiratory and cardiovascular health. Fraxinus pringlei is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk remedies.
